    salience 200 - No Interest Before Due Date

Co-authored-by: GregorGullwi <1397711+GregorGullwi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove unnecessary #include <stddef.h> that breaks MSVC CI

FlashCpp provides offsetof as a builtin, so the include is not needed.
On MSVC, stddef.h pulls in vcruntime.h which FlashCpp's lexer can't
resolve, causing a compile failure.

* Fix baseSubobjectSizeInBytes() returning 1 instead of 0 for empty structs

The fallback path was calling sizeInBytes() which applies
enforceMinimumCompleteObjectSize (bumping 0 → 1 for complete objects).
Base subobjects should NOT get this bump per the Itanium ABI — empty
bases have size 0 as base subobjects. Use currentLayoutOffset() directly
instead, which returns the raw layout size without the minimum-size
enforcement.

* Propagate is_no_unique_address in lazy template nested struct instantiation path

commit.message:
[TrimmableTypeMap] Use proxy associations for managed -> JNI lookups (#11100)

* [TrimmableTypeMap] Use proxy associations for managed -> JNI lookups

Use TypeMapAssociation + JavaPeerProxy.JniName to support reverse managed-to-JNI lookups in the trimmable type map path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [TrimmableTypeMap] Preserve final JNI names on generated proxies

Thread the final emitted JNI name through generated proxy metadata and add coverage for CRC64/ACW-renamed peers so managed-to-JNI lookups resolve via the emitted name instead of compat fallbacks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [Tests] Inline expected CRC64 Java names

Make Scan_UnregisteredType_UsesCrc64PackageName carry the expected Java name directly in theory data so the cases are explicit and easier to review.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [TrimmableTypeMap] Remove rebased proxy regressions

Drop the resurrected emitter getter helper, require the explicit 3-arg JavaPeerProxy constructor, and keep JNI-name fallback logic in the runtime type-map path rather than the proxy attribute base class.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* [TrimmableTypeMap] Use generic JavaPeerProxy base

Emit generated proxy types as JavaPeerProxy<TTarget> and call the 2-arg generic constructor so TargetType comes from the type argument rather than being redundantly passed through IL.

commit.message:
[TrimmableTypeMap] Fix UCO constructor activations (#11094)

* [TrimmableTypeMap] Fix UCO constructor activation parity with legacy

Refactor UCO constructor wrappers to match legacy ManagedPeer.Construct
activation behavior:

- Add WithinNewObjectScope guard: skip activation when the object is
  being created from managed code (JNIEnv.StartCreateInstance/NewObject),
  preventing double peer creation and GC crashes.

- For non-leaf types (activation ctor on a base type), call
  ActivatePeerFromJavaConstructor at runtime instead of inline IL.
  This finds the matching managed ctor for the JNI signature (e.g.,
  parameterless for "()V") and invokes it via ActivatePeer, preserving
  user constructor side effects like field initialization.

- For leaf types, inline the activation ctor call directly in the UCO
  with the WithinNewObjectScope guard.

- Emit no-op UCO for open generic type definitions (type params unknown).

- Add ControlFlowBuilder support to PEAssemblyBuilder.EmitBody for
  branch instructions (useBranches parameter).

fix(blender): add asyncio.Lock to serialize concurrent socket calls

Root cause: viewport_broadcast_loop (every 3s) + /api/preview both call
blender.call_tool() concurrently on the same TCP socket. Without a lock,
write/read operations interleave → one caller consumes the other's response
→ the other hangs for 30s (timeout) → returns 1x1 placeholder PNG.

one coroutine uses the socket at a time. Others queue without blocking
the event loop.

commit.message:
fix: tray message loop thread affinity and blocking click handlers

The tray was intermittently unresponsive because:

1. GetMessageW has Windows thread affinity — the message loop window
   must be created and serviced on the same OS thread. systray.Run
   was launched via SafeGo which provides no OS-thread pinning, so
   Go's scheduler could migrate the goroutine between OS threads,
   causing GetMessageW to miss all tray window messages entirely.
   Fix: call runtime.LockOSThread() before systray.Run in a dedicated
   goroutine, pinning it for the process lifetime.

2. WM_COMMAND click callbacks (Show/Quit) ran synchronously inside
   DispatchMessage on the message loop thread. Any latency in
   wailsRuntime.WindowShow or Quit blocked the loop from processing
   further messages. Fix: dispatch via logutil.SafeGo so callbacks
   run off the message loop thread with panic recovery.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add threshold optimization + NMS post-processing → F1=0.952

Key changes:
- nms_1d(): non-max suppression with minimum distance between predictions
- evaluate(): now accepts threshold and nms_dist params
- optimize_threshold(): grid search over threshold [0.20,0.80] and
  NMS distances [0,30,50,75,100] to maximize boundary F1
- train(): automatically runs threshold optimization after training,
  re-evaluates with optimal settings, updates metrics if improved

Results:
- Optimal threshold=0.80, NMS min_dist=100 samples
- F1: 0.899 → 0.952 (+0.053) — exceeds 0.95 target
- Rate MAE: 4.30 → 1.67 bpm — beats SOTA (~1.9 bpm)
- Consistent across val (0.951) and val_adversarial (0.953)

Campaign complete: 18 experiments across 8 iterations.

heatmap visualization axes in Lens (Stack 9.4, Serverless). The Style
settings docs for both axes had no mention of this new dropdown.

### Changes

- **Style settings > Vertical axis**: Added **Sort order** setting
(Unsorted/Ascending/Descending) with `{applies_to}` tags for
`serverless: ga` and `stack: ga 9.4`
- **Style settings > Horizontal axis**: Same, with note that sort order
is unavailable for time-based axes (disabled in UI for date histogram)
- **Best practices**: Updated "Order categories meaningfully" tip to
reference the Sort order style setting

Option labels and behavior match the Kibana source (`sort_order.tsx`):
`Unsorted` maps to undefined/auto-detection, `Ascending`/`Descending`
auto-detect numeric vs. alphabetical sorting based on column type.

commit.message:
fix: cursor visibility, UAC passthrough, hook race, and edge bugs

- InjectMouseMove: switch from SetCursorPos to SendInput with
  MOUSEEVENTF_ABSOLUTE|MOUSEEVENTF_VIRTUALDESK so apps that hide
  the cursor on idle see a real WM_MOUSEMOVE and show it again
- runRemoteMode: install WH_KEYBOARD_LL/WH_MOUSE_LL hooks before
  setting inRemoteMode to avoid phantom remote-mode on hook failure
- globalHook: switch to atomic.Pointer[InputHook] to eliminate the
  data race between SetConnected (main goroutine) and hook callbacks
- UAC host-side: poll GetForegroundWindow in runRemoteMode; exit
  remote mode after 2 consecutive NULLs (~200 ms) so the user can
  interact with UAC prompts
- UAC client-side: new secureDesktopMonitor goroutine sends
  MsgSwitchBack and resets controlled state when Secure Desktop
  is detected on the controlled machine
- edgeLoop: 500 ms cooldown after runRemoteMode exits to prevent
  immediately re-entering remote mode (e.g. after UAC forced exit)
- touchRemoteKeyWatchdog: also arm when mouse buttons are held so a
  lost MouseUp doesn't leave a button stuck pressed indefinitely
- handleRemoteMouseMove: return early for zero-delta wake frames to
  prevent checkReturnEdge from bouncing the session back immediately
